rationale: Author-of-record: the commit delivers exactly Bead 2's prescribed steps, no missing step and no unrequested scope. The changed set is precisely Bead 2's seven key_file_paths (verified via git show --name-only). Step-by-step: (1) ms-panel.js has the args input, full input hardening (path-element slug/spec/bead_id/mix.family, branch-name-safe target grammar with git check-ref-format + shell-metachar guards, positive-int round) run at entry before any command, the static ALLOWED_CLI literal array + four-identifier destructure, codex read-only sandbox pin, and the buildCommand chokepoint with per-verb fixed-flag templates + leading-dash value guard; sha is advisory-only and never read. (2) Registration via buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE,...), panel dir captured once from reported stdout, no hand-typed panel.json. (3) pipeline() fan-out, flattenMix, claude/codex slots with schema + self-Write, .codex.log persistence, exactly-one-object rule, and the same-reviewer re-serialize->MISSING ladder with substitution reserved to quota. (4) Deterministic quota-wall substitution honoring the flag (false->missing), then verbatim verify+tally return; no complete/consolidation/panel.json mutation. (5) byte-identical mirror + WorkflowFiles embed + installWorkflows Claude-only + 14->15 created-count. (6) both structural tests + install test. Doc-sync region present. Build clean; ./plugins/mindspec + ./internal/setup green; copies byte-identical.
findings: Exact scope: git show --name-only yields precisely Bead 2's 7 key_file_paths — no extra file, no missing file.

All Bead 2 steps 1-6 + doc-sync map cleanly onto the diff; every load-bearing invariant (ALLOWED_CLI static array, buildCommand chokepoint with template-fixed flags + leading-dash value guard, entry-time hardening, R3b anti-laundering ladder) is implemented as prescribed.

Branch-base context honored: workflow invokes `mindspec panel create/verify/tally` as runtime strings (110 not on this base); structural Go tests are static analysis and pass here; created-count is base+1 (14->15) not the plan's literal 14->15-from-a-different-base — consistent.

One args-contract nuance (non-blocking, fully disclosed): the workflow adds `claude_sub_on_quota` to args beyond spec R1's illustrative tuple {slug,spec,target,bead_id?,round,sha?,lenses,mix}. This is forced by the documented workflow limit (no fs/shell access -> the script cannot read .mindspec/config.yaml, and `mindspec config show` is intentionally outside ALLOWED_CLI), fail-closed when absent, and the interfaces.md region explicitly notes it is not part of R1's tuple. It trips no R1 falsification clause (mix still derives only from `mix`). Bead 3's plan step-1 invocation tuple omits this flag — a cross-bead item for the Bead 3 panel, not a Bead 2 defect.

The structured result carries verifyOutput/tallyOutput verbatim (unmodified CLI stdout) plus panelDir + per-slot outcomes for the caller's bookkeeping — consistent with the plan's 'single structured result' and 'pass panelDir forward' language; does not alter/paraphrase the verbatim stdout (R5 preserved).
Verified locally: go build ./... exit 0; go test ./plugins/mindspec ./internal/setup all ok; diff -q of the two ms-panel.js copies -> BYTE-IDENTICAL.
